model_answer: class Solution: def minimumSum(self, n: int, k: int) -> int: # We can take numbers 1..floor(k/2) safely. t = k // 2 if n <= t: return n * (n + 1) // 2 # Otherwise, take all 1..t and then start from k upwards. sum_small = t * (t + 1) // 2 r = n - t sum_large = r * (2 * k + (r - 1)) // 2 return sum_small + sum_large

question: determine-the-minimum-sum-of-a-k-avoiding-array You are given two integers, n and k. An array of distinct positive integers is called a k-avoiding array if there does not exist any pair of distinct elements that sum t...
model_answer: class Solution: def minimumSum(self, n: int, k: int) -> int: # We can safely take all numbers from 1 to floor(k/2) # because no two distinct numbers from this set will sum to k. m = min(n, k // 2) # count of small numbers we take # Sum of first m natural numbers small_sum = m * (m + 1) // 2 # Remaining numbers to pick, starting from k upwards remaining = n - m # Sum of arithmetic sequence: k, k+1, ..., k+remaining-1 big_sum = remaining * (2 * k + (remaining - 1)) // 2 return small_sum + big_sum

model_answer: class Solution: def minMaxSubarraySum(self, nums: List[int], k: int) -> int: total = 0 n = len(nums) # For each starting position for i in range(n): min_val = nums[i] max_val = nums[i] # For each ending position (subarray length from 1 to k) for j in range(i, min(i + k, n)): min_val = min(min_val, nums[j]) max_val = max(max_val, nums[j]) total += min_val + max_val return total

model_answer: class Solution: def hasTrailingZeros(self, nums: List[int]) -> bool: # A number has at least one trailing zero in binary iff it is even. # The bitwise OR of selected numbers is even iff ALL selected numbers are even. # So we just need to check if there are at least two even numbers. even_count = sum(1 for x in nums if x % 2 == 0) return even_count >= 2

question: count-of-substrings-containing-every-vowel-and-k-consonants-i You are given a string word and a non-negative integer k. Return the total number of substrings of word that contain every vowel ('a', 'e', 'i', 'o', and '...
model_answer: class Solution: def countOfSubstrings(self, word: str, k: int) -> int: n = len(word) vowel_idx = {'a':0, 'e':1, 'i':2, 'o':3, 'u':4} ALL = (1 << 5) - 1 res = 0 for i in range(n): mask = 0 cons = 0 for j in range(i, n): ch = word[j] if ch in vowel_idx: mask |= (1 << vowel_idx[ch]) else: cons += 1 if cons > k: break if cons == k and mask == ALL: res += 1 return res

model_answer: class Solution: def countOfSubstrings(self, word: str, k: int) -> int: n = len(word) vowels = set("aeiou") res = 0 for i in range(n): # counts of vowels from i..j vcount = {ch: 0 for ch in vowels} consonants = 0 for j in range(i, n): ch = word[j] if ch in vowels: vcount[ch] += 1 else: consonants += 1 if consonants > k: break # further extension only adds more consonants # check if all vowels are present at least once if consonants == k and all(vcount[v] > 0 for v in vowels): res += 1 return res
